name: SilverStripe CMS REST API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP CMS and framework with REST API — enabling development teams to build content-managed websites with a flexible data model, headless delivery via GraphQL (via SilverStripe GraphQL module), a developer-friendly ORM, and a content author-friendly editing interface, widely used in government, education, and enterprise in Australia and New Zealand.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via SilverStripe REST API for decoupled frontend applications
Managing SilverStripe pages and data objects via API for content migration automation
Querying SilverStripe content via GraphQL module for type-safe frontend queries
Building custom SilverStripe API endpoints via framework for specialized content delivery
Integrating SilverStripe with external systems via REST API for enterprise content workflows
not_for: Teams without PHP expertise (SilverStripe is a PHP framework/CMS)
Non-technical editors needing simple managed CMS without developer setup
US/EU teams with no ANZ presence (SilverStripe ecosystem strongest in ANZ)
best_when: A PHP development agency or ANZ government/education team needs an enterprise-grade open-source CMS with strong data modeling, GraphQL headless delivery, and a long track record in regulated/government environments.
avoid_when: You're outside the ANZ ecosystem and want a larger community, don't have PHP expertise, or need a managed SaaS CMS.

auth.notes: SilverStripe REST API uses bearer token auth via SilverStripe framework security token. Basic auth supported for development. OAuth2 available via SilverStripe OAuth module (contrib). GraphQL via SilverStripe GraphQL module (core v5+). No built-in webhooks. PHP session-based auth for admin. Member/Group-based permissions system. Custom API endpoints use SilverStripe's permission model.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (BSD license)
pricing.paid_tiers: SilverStripe Cloud (managed) pricing available; agency support from SilverStripe Ltd
pricing.notes: New Zealand company. Founded 2006 in Wellington by Sigurd Magnusson and Sam Minnee. BSD license. 15,000+ websites. Strong in Australian and New Zealand government and education. SilverStripe framework is the underlying PHP MVC (used independently of CMS). CWP (Common Web Platform) — NZ government shared hosting platform built on SilverStripe. Silverstripe Ltd acquired by Beca Group (NZ engineering firm) 2018. Active community in ANZ. Used by NZ Government, ANZ Bank, Vodafone NZ. SilverStripe 4 (current stable) with GraphQL 4 support.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API via SilverStripe framework + GraphQL via module. Bearer token or basic auth. No webhooks. Open source. PHP expertise required. ANZ-focused community. Self-hosted. No rate limits from platform. GraphQL requires separate module installation and schema definition.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads idempotent by ID or URL segment. GraphQL queries idempotent. Write operations via custom endpoints require custom idempotency. REST reads fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: PHP expertise required — not for non-PHP developers
No built-in HTTP webhooks — requires custom extension
GraphQL requires SilverStripe GraphQL module installation (not default)
ANZ-ecosystem community — less global community support than Laravel/Drupal
Self-hosted — agents need network access to the SilverStripe server

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. Bearer token auth. PHP security model. SilverStripe framework security team patches vulnerabilities. Used by NZ Government — government-grade security requirements influence development. Group-based permissions system. Open source — auditable. No OAuth2 out-of-box — requires contrib module.
